title: We Cut 94% of AI Coding Tokens With a Local Code Index - Rajkumar Sakthivel, Tesco
summary: Tesco built a local code retrieval layer using AST-aware chunking and hybrid vector/keyword search to reduce tokens sent to AI coding models from 45,000 to 5,000 per query. The system achieved 94% token reduction with faster responses and improved accuracy by optimizing context selection instead of model parameters.
writeup: This talk from Tesco's Rajkumar Sakthivel details a local code retrieval layer that cut tokens sent to AI coding agents by 94%. The architecture combines AST-aware chunking, vector search paired with keyword matching, and lightweight relationship tracking, with LLM-based scoring backed by heuristic fallbacks to manage retrieval quality. The central argument is that optimizing the context fed to a model matters more than tuning the model itself, which directly addresses the cost and efficiency pressures of running coding agents at scale.
